text: This BBC report covers the UK government’s announcement that it is almost certain Russia was responsible for a cyberattack against Viasat’s KA-SAT satellite internet service around the start of the invasion of Ukraine. The article describes outages affecting several thousand Ukrainian customers and wind farms and internet users across central Europe, quoting officials who call the incident a deliberate and malicious attempt to disrupt communications. It situates the event within a broader pattern of Russian cyber operations linked to the conflict and notes that public attribution followed joint assessments by the UK, US and EU.
